issue.title: Wrong code at -O2/3 on x86-64_linux-gnu (recent regression)
issue.author: shao-hua-li

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Looks like poison flags don't get cleared as expected.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Minimized:
```llvm
define i32 @test(i64 %x) {
  %and = and i64 %x, 1
  %trunc = trunc i64 %and to i32
  %or = or disjoint i32 %trunc, -2
  ret i32 %or
}
```
The `%and` gets dropped but `disjoint` is kept.

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
@nikic, could you kindly elaborate this? I'm having a hard time getting this, the transformation looks correct with simple `or` (https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/ui3yFf). Semantics for `disjoint`:
> If the disjoint flag is set, and the or has a one and the same bit in both arguments, the result is poison.

However, here:
```llvm
  %add = add nuw nsw i64 %conv5, %conv4
  %and = and i64 %add, 1
  %2 = trunc i64 %and to i32
  %conv7 = or disjoint i32 %2, -2
```
The two or operands do not seem to have any same bit in both arguments.

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body: OK, I got it now, the `disjoint` should not be there at all after the transformation. Makes sense, thank you.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Fixed another issue I noticed in this code in https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/cb6240d247b3419dea29eb99261171ea239b1c5c.

history.commit_subject: [BDCE] Handle multi-use binary ops upon demanded bits
history.commit_message:
[BDCE] Handle multi-use binary ops upon demanded bits

Simplify multi-use `and`/`or`/`xor` when these last
do not affect the demanded bits being considered.

Fixes: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/78596.

Proofs: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/EjuWHa.

tests.test_body:
define void @select_vectorized(i1 %c, <2 x i8> %a, <2 x i8> %b) {
entry:
  %and1 = and <2 x i8> %a, splat (i8 28)
  %and2 = and <2 x i8> %b, splat (i8 29)
  %s = select i1 %c, <2 x i8> %and1, <2 x i8> %and2
  %ret1 = and <2 x i8> %s, splat (i8 4)
  %ret2 = and <2 x i8> %s, splat (i8 12)
  call void @use3(<2 x i8> %ret1)
  call void @use3(<2 x i8> %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use3(<2 x i8>)

define void @and(i64 %a) {
entry:
  %and = and i64 %a, 24
  %ret1 = and i64 %and, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %and, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @select_of_and_multiuse(i1 %c, i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %and1 = and i64 %a, 24
  %and2 = and i64 %b, 25
  %s = select i1 %c, i64 %and1, i64 %and2
  %ret1 = and i64 %s, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %s, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use2(i64 %ret2, i64 %and2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

declare void @use2(i64, i64)

define void @or_disjoint_of_and(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %and1 = and i64 %a, 56
  %and2 = and i64 %b, 25
  %or = or disjoint i64 %and1, %and2
  %ret1 = and i64 %or, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %or, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @select_of_xor(i1 %c, i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %xor1 = xor i64 %a, 128
  %xor2 = xor i64 %b, 36
  %s = select i1 %c, i64 %xor1, i64 %xor2
  %ret1 = and i64 %s, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %s, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @select_of_or(i1 %c, i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %or1 = or i64 %a, 3
  %or2 = or i64 %b, 192
  %s = select i1 %c, i64 %or1, i64 %or2
  %ret1 = and i64 %s, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %s, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @select_of_and(i1 %c, i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %and1 = and i64 %a, 24
  %and2 = and i64 %b, 25
  %s = select i1 %c, i64 %and1, i64 %and2
  %ret1 = and i64 %s, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %s, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @xor(i64 %a) {
entry:
  %xor = xor i64 %a, 3
  %ret1 = and i64 %xor, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %xor, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @select_of_and_2(i1 %c, i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %and1 = and i64 %a, 25
  %and2 = and i64 %b, 23
  %s = select i1 %c, i64 %and1, i64 %and2
  %ret1 = and i64 %s, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %s, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @or_of_and(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  %and1 = and i64 %a, 24
  %and2 = and i64 %b, 25
  %or = or i64 %and1, %and2
  %ret1 = and i64 %or, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %or, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

define void @or(i64 %a) {
entry:
  %or = or i64 %a, 3
  %ret1 = and i64 %or, 8
  %ret2 = and i64 %or, 16
  call void @use(i64 %ret1)
  call void @use(i64 %ret2)
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i64)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare <2 x i32> @llvm.fshr.v2i32(<2 x i32>, <2 x i32>, <2 x i32>) #0

define <2 x i32> @pr39771_fshr_multi_use_instr_vec(<2 x i32> %a) {
  %x = or <2 x i32> %a, splat (i32 2)
  %b = tail call <2 x i32> @llvm.fshr.v2i32(<2 x i32> %x, <2 x i32> %x, <2 x i32> splat (i32 1))
  %c = lshr <2 x i32> %b, splat (i32 23)
  %d = xor <2 x i32> %c, %b
  %e = and <2 x i32> %d, splat (i32 31)
  ret <2 x i32> %e
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.fshr.i32(i32, i32, i32) #0

define i32 @pr39771_fshr_multi_use_instr(i32 %a) {
  %x = or i32 %a, 2
  %b = tail call i32 @llvm.fshr.i32(i32 %x, i32 %x, i32 1)
  %c = lshr i32 %b, 23
  %d = xor i32 %c, %b
  %e = and i32 %d, 31
  ret i32 %e
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }
